name: Coannihilation dark matter
summary: Thermal-relic dark matter whose abundance is determined not (or not solely) by chi-chi self-annihilation, but by the joint freeze-out of chi together with a slightly heavier partner X (with mass splitting Delta m / m ~ few percent) that co-annihilates more efficiently. The relic abundance is exponentially sensitive to Delta m. Mechanism resolves the tension between LZ constraints (which push sigma_chi_chi down) and the observed Omega_DM (which requires sigma_eff ~ pb).

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12 for chi-X mass splittings ~ 5-15% even when direct chi-chi annihilation is below the standard WIMP cross-section. Major escape route from LZ exclusion for MSSM neutralino DM (bino-stop, bino-stau, bino-wino coannihilation regions remain alive).

touches_benchmarks.note: By construction allows arbitrarily low direct-detection cross-section while still saturating relic abundance via coannihilation; this is precisely the loophole that keeps MSSM neutralino DM alive.
Late-time annihilation can be suppressed: at v -> 0 only chi-chi contributes (X has decayed); indirect signal can be far below dwarf-galaxy bounds.

introduces.note: Builds on existing DM frameworks. New parameters: partner mass Delta m, partner-SM couplings.

history: Griest and Seckel (1991), 'Three exceptions in the calculation of relic abundances', Phys. Rev. D 43, 3191 — established the framework. Extensively used in MSSM dark-matter phenomenology (Edsjo-Gondolo 1997; modern MicrOMEGAs / DarkSUSY codes implement coannihilation by default).

references.citation: Griest and Seckel, 'Three exceptions in the calculation of relic abundances', Phys. Rev. D 43, 3191 (1991)
